question: Why is M3 a system-on-chip?
reference_answer: It integrates CPU, GPU and other engines.
student_answer: It combines CPU, GPU, memory controllers and specialist engines on one chip.
label: excellent

question: Why is M3 a system-on-chip?
reference_answer: It integrates CPU, GPU and other engines.
student_answer: It is a fast CPU made by Apple.
label: partial

question: Explain unified memory.
reference_answer: CPU and GPU share a physical memory pool; synchronization still matters.
student_answer: CPU and GPU can access one shared pool, reducing copies, but they still need synchronization.
label: excellent

question: Explain unified memory.
reference_answer: CPU and GPU share a physical memory pool; synchronization still matters.
label: incorrect

question: Give a safe optimization workflow.
reference_answer: Benchmark, profile, change, verify and measure again.
student_answer: First measure a representative baseline, profile the bottleneck, make one change, test correctness, then benchmark again.
label: excellent

question: Give a safe optimization workflow.
reference_answer: Benchmark, profile, change, verify and measure again.
label: incorrect

question: Why is a data race unsafe?
reference_answer: Speed does not provide ordering; synchronization is required.
student_answer: Multiple cores can access conflicting data without a happens-before relation, so use atomics or locks.
label: excellent

question: When should Metal compute be used?
reference_answer: For large parallel work when benefits exceed dispatch costs.
student_answer: Use it for parallel workloads after profiling shows GPU benefits outweigh overhead.
label: excellent
